working: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/14) is the standout: +76.5% traffic lift over baseline (17.0 -> 30.0 daily sessions) across 9,731 sends with a 48.2% open rate.
Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3) lifted traffic +65.2% (21.0 -> 34.7 daily sessions) with a 45.9% open — the reminder-send cadence keeps working.
Open rates are excellent: 52.15% average across 100 campaigns, far above the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark.
Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) had the best engagement quality: 1.79% click rate and 3.77% click-to-open, the highest of the 12 in-window sends.
Organic Search share ticked up to 36.4% (795 sessions) from 35.4% last run — SEO is slowly taking share from other channels.
not_working: Total UTM leakage: all 12 in-window sends show email_sessions_3d = 0 while logging 21-76 direct_sessions_3d each — every newsletter click is being mis-credited to Direct (55.5% of all traffic) because links carry no UTM tags.
Clicks lag opens badly: 2.07% average click rate and click-to-open mostly 1-2% (benchmark ~10%) — subscribers open but the content isn't driving them to the site.
Several sends produced negative lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 -50.2%, Reminder 2: Alumni eNews - March -31.1%, Movement Reminder: Staff-Client March -26.7%, Alumni eNews - June -9.8%.
Organic Social is flat at 1.6% (35 sessions) and AI Assistant traffic is 1 session — both channels are effectively dormant.
GSC: only 4 clicks on 1,706 impressions (0.23% CTR) at an impression-weighted position of 18.8, slightly worse than 18.6 last run; window sessions dipped 3.2% (2,257 -> 2,184).
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to every newsletter link — this is the #1 fix; it will reclassify the email share of the 55.5% Direct bucket and make lift measurement trustworthy.

Keep the mid-week reminder pattern: the two best lifts came from a Thursday Staff-Client send (5/14, +76.5%) and a Wednesday final-reminder (6/3, +65.2%); initial weekend/Monday alumni sends underperformed (5/2 -50.2%).
Repromote Staff-Client content themes — both lift leaders were Staff-Client or its reminder; the larger 9.4-9.7K list converts attention into site visits better than the 1K alumni list.
Attack the click gap: with 52% opens but ~2% clicks, add prominent above-the-fold CTAs linking to bariatriccounselingcenter.com pages in every send.
SEO follow-up: avg position slipped to 18.8 and clicks fell 5 -> 4; prioritize on-page work for the 23 tracked keywords with data to move pages from page 2 toward page 1.
summary: 90-day window logged 2,184 sessions (down 3.2% vs the 6/10 run), with Direct at 55.5% still inflated by untagged newsletter clicks — UTM tagging remains the single highest-impact fix. Email opens stay strong (52% avg) and the 5/14 Staff-Client and 6/3 reminder sends drove +77%/+65% traffic lifts, but ~2% click rates and a slipping GSC position (18.8) cap the upside.
